name: Maldives
visa: Free 30-day visa on arrival for most nationalities
known_for: Overwater bungalows, coral reefs, crystal-clear lagoons, marine life

name: Bali, Indonesia
visa: Visa on arrival available for most nationalities ($35 USD)
known_for: Hindu temples, rice terraces, surfing, spiritual wellness, nightlife

name: Dubai, UAE
visa: Free visa on arrival for most nationalities (check requirements)
known_for: Skyscrapers, luxury shopping, desert safaris, gold souks, Burj Khalifa

name: Santorini & Mykonos, Greece
visa: Schengen visa required for Turkish passport holders
known_for: Whitewashed villages, caldera sunsets, parties, ancient ruins, wine

name: Morocco
known_for: Sahara desert, medinas, riads, Berber culture, souks, tagine

name: Japan
visa: Visa required for Turkish passport (apply at Japanese consulate)
climate: Temperate; cherry blossom Apr: 10–18°C; summer: hot & humid
known_for: Cherry blossoms, temples, anime, cuisine, bullet trains, Mount Fuji

name: Tanzania & Zanzibar
known_for: Serengeti, Big 5, wildebeest migration, Kilimanjaro, Zanzibar beaches

name: Thailand
known_for: Temples, beaches, street food, elephants, nightlife, island hopping

name: Switzerland & France
visa: Schengen visa required for Turkish passport holders
climate: Alpine: cool to cold; Paris: mild 15–25°C summer
known_for: Alps, skiing, chocolate, watches, Eiffel Tower, Louvre, wine

name: Egypt
visa: Visa on arrival ($25 USD) or e-visa
climate: Oct–Apr: 18–28°C; May–Sep: extremely hot (40°C+) especially in Luxor
known_for: Pyramids, pharaonic history, Nile, Red Sea diving, bazaars

name: Iceland
visa: Schengen visa required for Turkish passport holders
known_for: Northern Lights, geysers, waterfalls, glaciers, Blue Lagoon, puffins

name: Peru & Argentina
visa: Visa-free for Turkish passport in both countries (check current status)
climate: Lima: mild 18–25°C; Cusco: cold nights at altitude; Buenos Aires: temperate
known_for: Machu Picchu, Inca Trail, Buenos Aires tango, Iguazu Falls, Patagonia

name: Istanbul
visa: No visa needed for Turkish citizens; e-visa or visa-on-arrival for most international visitors
climate: Mediterranean-influenced; summers 28–34°C, winters 3–10°C with occasional snow
known_for: Hagia Sophia, Topkapi Palace, Bosphorus, Grand Bazaar, Galata Tower, Turkish cuisine, hammams, historic mosques

name: Sapanca & Kartepe
climate: Mild and green; spring 15–22°C; summer up to 30°C; snowy winters at Kartepe elevation
known_for: Lake Sapanca (freshwater lake), Kartepe ski resort, Maşukiye forest restaurants and waterfalls, thermal springs, peaceful nature escapes from Istanbul (90 min)

name: Bursa
climate: Temperate; spring/autumn mild 15–22°C; Uludağ peak: snowy Nov–Apr
known_for: Ottoman capital heritage, Grand Mosque (Ulu Cami), Green Mosque and Tomb, Uludağ ski resort, İskender kebab, silk market (Koza Han), thermal baths, Cumalıkızık village (UNESCO)

name: Trabzon & Eastern Black Sea
climate: Humid and green year-round; summers 20–28°C; heavy rainfall Nov–Mar
known_for: Sümela Monastery (UNESCO-listed cliff monastery), Uzungöl mountain lake, Ayder Plateau, Black Sea tea gardens, hamsi (anchovy) cuisine, Atatürk's Villa, Rize tea plantations

name: Cappadocia & Pamukkale
best_season: Apr–Jun, Sep–Oct (balloon flights; avoid winter fog)
climate: Continental; Cappadocia: hot summers 30°C+, cold winters -5°C; Pamukkale: mild Mediterranean
known_for: Cappadocia hot air balloons, underground cities, cave hotels, Göreme Open Air Museum, Pamukkale travertine terraces, Hierapolis ancient ruins
